hero.headline: 3.05 Million Contracts. One Question:
hero.sub: RUBLI tracks every Mexican federal procurement contract since 2002. Powered by ML risk scoring, network analysis, and 748 documented corruption cases.
hero.heroSubtitle: Mexican federal procurement data analyzed by machine learning to identify risk patterns across 12 government sectors
hero.riskDisclaimer: Risk scores indicate statistical similarity to documented corruption patterns — not proof of wrongdoing.
hero.storySubtitle: RUBLI analyzes 3.1 million federal procurement contracts using machine learning to surface corruption patterns, ghost companies, and market manipulation.
hero.whatIs: The largest open analysis of Mexican federal procurement. 23 years of contract data. One risk model. Every score explained.
hero.credibility: Source: COMPRANET 2002-2025  |  Model v0.6.5  |  AUC 0.828  |  Updated March 2026

ctaPanels.sub: Choose where to begin — every entry point leads to data.

cases.desc: ~748 documented corruption cases with linked contracts and vendors

story1.headline: 81.9% of contracts are awarded without competition.
story1.body: Direct award triples the OECD maximum (25%). The pattern is structural: in 2023, 9 out of every 10 pesos spent avoided public tender.

story2.headline: 184K contracts at critical risk.
story2.body: Our v0.6.5 model (AUC 0.828) flags 6% of contracts as critical — statistically similar to 748 documented corruption cases.

story3.headline: 320 vendors under immediate investigation.
story3.body: ARIA — our automated intelligence engine — prioritizes T1 vendors: highest concentration, lowest institutional diversity, multiple capture signals.

recentFlags.label: Live alerts
recentFlags.headline: Latest critical flags
recentFlags.error: Could not load recent flags. Backend may be offline.

how.title: From Raw Data to Investigation Leads
how.sub: Three steps. 9 active ML features. 12 per-sector models.
how.step1_body: 23 years of COMPRANET procurement data -- 3.1 million contracts, 320K vendors, 12 sectors. Cleaned, classified, and normalized.
how.step2_body: ML risk scoring with 9 active z-score features, per-sector logistic models, PU-learning correction. Train AUC 0.798, Test AUC 0.828.
how.step3_body: Ranked investigation queue with evidence, SHAP explanations, confidence intervals. Every score has a reason.
how.label: HOW WE DETECT PATTERNS
how.headline: From raw data to risk intelligence
how.step01Body: 3.05M contracts from COMPRANET (2002-2025), cleaned and standardized
how.step02Body: Vendor concentration, atypical prices, direct awards, network patterns...
how.step03Body: Compared against 748 documented corruption cases across all 12 sectors
how.step04Body: We grade procurement health from Excellent to Critical for every sector
how.disclaimer: Scores are statistical risk indicators, not proof of corruption. A high score means the contract resembles documented corruption patterns.

reportCard.label: OUR NATIONAL GRADE
reportCard.headline: How healthy is Mexican public procurement?

aria.label: AUTOMATED RISK ANALYSIS
aria.headline: Risk Analysis Briefing
aria.body: Our 9-module Automated Risk Investigation Algorithm classifies 318K vendors into investigation tiers, detects monopoly, ghost company, and institutional capture patterns, and cross-references against SAT, SFP, and ASF registries.

cta.title: Open-source. No account required.
cta.sub: Data from COMPRANET 2002-2025. Model v0.6.5. AUC 0.828.
cta.headline: Start investigating
cta.body: Browse contracts, investigate vendors, and discover risk patterns.
cta.redThreadTeaser: Follow the investigation trail on Laboratorios Pisa

featuredCase.label: DOCUMENTED CASE
featuredCase.description: This pharmaceutical company received government contracts with {{directAwardPct}}% direct awards. The risk model assigns a score of {{riskScore}} — the highest possible — indicating its contracting pattern closely resembles documented corruption cases.

footer.platform: RUBLI -- Mexican Government Procurement Analysis Platform

revelation.dek: Twenty-three years of Mexican federal procurement, opened for public investigation.
revelation.ledeBody: Every peso the Mexican federal government spent between 2002 and 2025, traced, classified and scored against 748 documented corruption cases.

investigations.headline: Where to begin
investigations.sub: Three entry points into the data. Each leads to documented evidence.

one.headline: IMSS and the 9,366 phantom contracts.
one.body: Two vendors — reputed fronts for a ghost network — collected thousands of contracts from the national health institute. Model score: 0.962 on average. Ninety-nine percent flagged critical.

two.headline: Where vendors charge three times the market rate.
two.body: Price volatility is the single strongest signal in the v0.6.5 model. In four sectors, the top quartile of vendors charges 3x the sector median on direct awards.

three.headline: 81.9% of federal spending escapes public tender.
three.body: The OECD integrity threshold for direct awards is 25%. Mexico sits at 81.9% — the highest rate in the OECD sample. The gap has widened every sexenio since 2006.

scorecards.headline: 2,563 federal institutions ranked by transparency
scorecards.sub: Annual accountability ranking — scored 0–100 across five pillars. Openness, price fairness, vendor diversity, process integrity, external alerts.

journalists.headline: Break the story before anyone else.
journalists.body: ARIA surfaces the highest-risk vendors — those scoring 0.80+ against 748 documented corruption cases. Each lead includes the evidence trail and financial impact.

researchers.headline: Test hypotheses with 23 years of data.
researchers.body: Download raw contract data, access z-score features, and replicate the v0.6.5 risk model. Full methodology with AUC metrics and ground truth documentation.

citizens.headline: See how your tax pesos were spent.
citizens.body: 3 million federal contracts, every sector, every administration since 2002. The Report Card grades each ministry on procurement health. No login required.
